title: Remove SELinux transition rule after conmon is started.
body:
We have an issue where iptables command is being executed by podman
and attempted to run with a different label. This fix changes podman
to only change the label on the conmon command and then set the
SELinux interface back to the default.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: @baude @mheon I believe this will fix the iptables error @umohnani8 and I were seeing this morning.
One nit on whitespace otherwise LGTM

I'm going to retract my earlier LGTM until we get OS thread pinning in here. I'd love to have @giuseppe give an LGTM on the implementation once it's ready given his experience with this in the userns code.

I am fine with the current implementation.  In CRI-O @mrunalp preferred to have a `defer runtime.UnlockOSThread()` when err == nil.  If the goroutine exits when the lock>0 then the os thread is destroyed and the Go runtime creates another one, the cost of creating a thread is very small and we don't risk to use the wrong SElinux context in another goroutine.
LGTM now that the thread locking changes are in

:pushpin: Commit 4ba6718 has been approved by `giuseppe`

:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
